text.title: ddd-repositories Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ddd-repositories. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The Repository interface MUST live in the domain layer and MUST NOT import any ORM or infrastructure types (no SQLAlchemy, Django ORM, JPA, EF). It MUST declare methods using only domain types: find_by_id(UUID) -> Optional[Aggregate], save(Aggregate) -> None, delete(Aggregate) -> None.
rule.rationale: ORM types in the domain interface couple the domain to the infrastructure. Swapping the ORM then requires changing domain code, which is the dependency inversion violation the Repository is designed to prevent.

rule.statement: The Repository MUST return fully reconstituted Aggregate objects, never ORM model instances. The infrastructure implementation is responsible for translating between persistence models and domain objects via _to_entity() / _to_model() methods.
rule.rationale: Returning ORM models leaks persistence metadata (lazy-loading proxies, change-tracking) into the domain. Domain code then depends on ORM lifecycle semantics instead of domain semantics.

rule.statement: Repositories MUST load Aggregates by identity only (find_by_id). Collection queries by arbitrary criteria MUST go in a separate Read Model or Query Service, never in the Repository interface.
rule.rationale: Adding rich query methods to the Repository blurs the boundary between the write model (Aggregates) and the read model (projections). It also encourages bypassing Aggregate invariants by loading partial state.
